author: P40L0
description:
[img]https://i.imgur.com/VXEqV6v.jpeg[/img]

[center][b][size=6][font=Lucida Sans Unicode]Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 - Ultimate Engine Tweaks[/font][/size][/b][/center]

After over [url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/P40L0X/mods]1 year of iterative work[/url] with 40+ other UE5 games, I would like to share my definitive, optimized, custom "Engine.ini" for the game.

My goal was to just include as many optimizations as possible (both for CPU/GPU/RAM/SSD) in order to remove most stuttering, improve performance, reduce input latency and improve picture clarity (e.g. by optimizing Film Grain, Motion Blur & removing Chromatic Aberration, optimizing DLSS/FSR Upscaling and Anti-Aliasing, reducing ghosting and shimmering) all without any visual loss nor introducing glitches or crashes.

If you want to know [u]exactly[/u] what every single optimization does and what their combined result is, have a look [url=https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1A71Q9jd0sm0MaCRWib8SJEY_urNk-Wuyl2pK5UI_4DI/edit?usp=sharing]here[/url]


[b]What to choose between the "VRR" and "No VRR" mod variants?[/b]
VRR stands for [url=https://www.rtings.com/tv/tests/motion/variable-refresh-rate]Variable Refresh Rate[/url]﻿, which can translate to G-Sync for NVIDIA or AMD Freesync for AMD.
You need to cross check if it is enabled both on your NVIDIA/AMD Control Panel and the Display itself (Monitor/TV)
If yes on both, use the "VRR" variant of the mod.
If no, use the "No VRR" variant instead.

Open the game at least once and close it, then you can directly download/extract my [b]Engine.ini[/b] to paste in:

[center]
[quote][i]%localappdata%\Sandfall\Saved\Config\WinGDK[/i][/quote]
[size=3]([u]Xbox / Game Pass version only[/u])[/size]

(Press Win + R , type the path above and click Enter to open the folder directly)

then Right-Click on it -> Properties -> make sure the "[b]Read-Only[/b]" box is selected -> Apply/OK

﻿[/center]

[center]

[u][size=3]For Steam & GOG versions only:[/size][/u]

Go and paste it into:

[quote][i]%localappdata%\Sandfall\Saved\Config\Windows[/i][/quote]
(Press Win + R , type the path above and click Enter to open the folder directly)

then Right-Click on it -> Properties -> make sure the "[b]Read-Only[/b]" box is selected -> Apply/OK


[b](To Uninstall just delete the Engine.ini, the game will use its defaults on next launch)


[/b][u]
DO NOT USE TOGETHER WITH OTHERS "OPTIMIZATION" MODS
[/u](any other mod will be compatible)

Actually I [b]highly recommend[/b] installing [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/mods/24]ClairObscurFix[/url]﻿ together with my mod.



For additional PC/Windows optimizations, I also highly recommend to follow and apply my:

[url=https://www.patreon.com/posts/88124101]PC Gaming: Optimized - The Ultimate Guide (including NVIDIA/AMD CP + G-Sync/Freesync + HDR Best Settings)[/url]

[size=3]
All of this is [b]FREE[/b] but, if you found it useful and want to support my work, please consider to:[/size] 

﻿[url=https://www.paypal.com/donate/?hosted_button_id=9N5Z6W6BDTWBE][img]https://i.imgur.com/XiHaimn.png[/img]﻿[/url]

or

[url=https://www.patreon.com/techoptimized][img]https://i.imgur.com/SihtTss.png[/img][/url]

[b]Thank you![/b] ﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿🙏🏻


Also don't forget to [url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/P40L0X/mods]check out my other mods[/url]﻿ ;)

Enjoy!
-P[/center]



[center][img]https://i.imgur.com/lQGjewH.png[/img]﻿﻿[/center]

Major UET overhaul with 20 new optimizations added, 15 already present optimizations fine-tuned and 50 deprecated and/or unstable tweaks removed
Performance and Smoothness are now noticeably improved for all PC configurations with no noticeable visual loss
Lower Input Latency (by consequence)
Lower VRAM usage
Stability improvements further diminishing the risk of crashes and fixing rare visual glitches some users reported on some PC configurations
This may very well be the last/definitive version of the mod :)

author: Caites
description:
[center][size=5][color=#ffd966][b]No Intro[/b][/color][/size][/center]
[quote][center][size=3][b][color=#b6d7a8]Clair Obscur v1.5.5 ready[/color][/b][/size][/center]
[/quote][size=3]
Mod skips epilepsy warning, save warning and 3 logos. That's your shortest way to main menu.

Please, do not use if you have epilepsy, you will miss the warning with this mod installed.[/size]

[color=#ffd966][size=4][b]Installation[/b][/size][/color]
[size=3]Unzip archive content to your game root folder. E[i]xpedition33_no_intro.bat[/i] file should appear in game root folder next to Expedition33_Steam.exe.

[color=#e06666] Use [b].bat[/b] for starting the game directly or make a shortcut on desktop.[/color]

Use one version, either for [b]Steam[/b] or [b]Gamepass[/b]. 

For Gamepass version if your Gamepass folder isn't XboxGames, change it in bat file.[/size]
[color=#ffd966][size=4][b]
Uninstallation[/b][/size][/color]
[size=3]Remove three [/size][size=3][i]000_NoIntro_P [/i][/size][size=3]files in \Expedition 33\Sandfall\Content\Paks\~mods and expedition33_no_intro.bat in game root folder.[/size]

[color=#ffd966][size=4][b]Compatibility[/b][/size][/color]
[size=3]Should be compatible with anything.

author: Caites
description:
[center][b][color=#ffd966][size=5]Easier or Harder Dodge and Parry[/size][/color]
[size=4](satellite mod - [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/mods/230]Easier Gradient Counter and Jump[/url]﻿)[/size][/b][/center]
[center][/center]
[center][quote][size=3][b][color=#b6d7a8]Clair Obscur v1.5.5 ready[/color][/b][/size][/quote][/center]

[size=3]Mod changes[b] immunity durations[/b] for dodge and parry up to [b]difficulty[/b] (Story/Expedition/Expert modes).[/size]

[quote][size=3]Here are [color=#93c47d]vanilla[/color] times for understanding:
Dodge: 0.5s/0.22s/0.22s [color=#999999](in release version 1.0 game had 0.3/0.22/0.22)[/color]
Parry: 0.35s/0.15s/0.15s [color=#999999](in release version 1.0 game had 0.22/0.15/0.15)[/color][/size][/quote][size=3]
Mod has 6 versions. Below are version names and invulnerability times in seconds for easy/mid/hard difficulty:[/size]

[quote][size=3][color=#fff2cc][b]Slightly Easier[/b] (+30% to parry/dodge window)[/color]:
Dodge: 0.4/0.35/0.3
Parry: 0.3/0.25/0.2[/size][/quote][quote][size=3][color=#ffe599][b]Easier[/b] (+100%):[/color]
Dodge: 0.7/0.44/0.44
Parry: 0.5/0.3/0.3[/size][/quote][quote][size=3][color=#f1c232][b]Even Easier[/b] (+200%):[/color]
Dodge: 1.05/0.66/0.66
Parry: 0.75/0.45/0.45[/size][/quote]
[quote][size=3][color=#e69138][b]Disgustingly Easy[/b] (+450%)[/color]:
Dodge: 1.6/1.4/1.2
Parry: 1.4/1.2/1.0[/size][/quote]
[quote][size=3][color=#a4c2f4][b]True Expert[/b] (with it Expert mode is actually harder than Expedition mode, unlike vanilla)[/color]:
Dodge: 0.35/0.22/0.18
Parry: 0.25/0.15/0.12[/size][/quote]
[quote][size=3][color=#b4a7d6][b]Gigamec[/b] (twice harder on Expert, true baguette spirit)[/color]:
Dodge: 0.3/0.2/0.1
Parry: 0.22/0.15/0.07[/size][/quote][size=3]
You can also find optional '[b]Slower QTE[/b]' version in Optional Files for more generous (0.6s -> 0.8s) manual attack QTE window. It will work with or without main parry/dodge version.[/size]

[color=#ffd966][size=4][b]Installation[/b][/size][/color]
[size=3]Unzip three [i]000_Easier_P[/i][/size] [size=3]files (pak/utoc/ucas) from archive to this game folder: \Steam\steamapps\common\Expedition 33\Sandfall\Content\Paks\~mods. Create ~mods folder if you don't have one yet.[/size]

[size=3]Install [b]one[/b] main version (for dodge and parry) and, optionally, addon for Skill QTE.

Path for [b]Gamepass[/b] users: C:\XboxGames\Clair Obscur- Expedition 33\Content\Sandfall\Content\Paks\~mods.[/size]

[color=#ffd966][size=4][b]Uninstallation[/b][/size][/color]
[size=3]Just remove three [/size][size=3][i]000_Easier_P [/i][/size][size=3]files.[/size]

[color=#ffd966][size=4][b]Compatibility[/b][/size][/color]
[size=3]Should be compatible with anything not affecting difficulty table.
Compatible with [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/mods/230]Easier Gradient Counter and Jump[/url].

[color=#ffd966][size=4][b]My Mods[/b][/size][/color][size=3]

[/url][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/mods/31]More Upgrade Materials

author: Greenylie
description:
[size=6][center][b]Introduction[/b][/center][/size][size=3]You must have noticed the extreme low bitrate in which the 15 pre-rendered cutscenes present themselves on screen. With this mod, I tried to give them justice by upscaling, removing artifacts and interpolating the frames with AI.[/size]
[size=6][b][left][size=5]Imgsli Albums
[/size][/left][/b][/size][list]
[*][size=3][url=https://imgsli.com/Mzc1MTg5]My Flower (Intro)[/url][/size]
[/list][list]
[*][size=3][url=https://imgsli.com/Mzc1MTg4]The Gommage[/url][/size]
[/list][list]
[*][size=3][url=https://imgsli.com/Mzc1MTky]The Departure[/url][/size]
[/list][list]
[*][size=3][url=https://imgsli.com/Mzc1MTkw]The End of Expedition 33[/url][/size]
[/list][list]
[*][size=3][url=https://imgsli.com/Mzc1MTk1]Maelle Nightmare (Scream)[/url][/size]
[/list][size=2][i]Will add more eventually... (To avoid spoilers)
Note: These are for the 1080p version[/i][/size]

[size=6][center][b]Installation[/b][/center][/size][size=5][b]For 1080p Version:[/b][b]
[/b][/size][list=1]
[*][size=3]Open the game folder and navigate to Sandfall/Content/Paks[/size]
[*][size=3]If that's the first mod you download, create a folder named ~mods (Sandfall/Content/Paks/~mods)[/size]
[*][size=3]Place the downloaded .pak file ~COE33ImprovedCinematics_P.pak inside the ~mods folder[/size]
[*][size=3]You should end up with Sandfall/Content/Paks/~mods/~COE33ImprovedCinematics_P.pak[/size]
[/list]
[size=5][b]For 4K Version:[/b]
[/size][list=1]
[*][size=3]Follow steps 1-4 from the 1080p installation above (With the 4k version .zip file)[/size]
[*][size=3]Do the same for the 1.3 Patch which fixes two cutscenes (You can find it in updates), both files need to be in the ~mods directory.[/size]
[/list][size=6][center][b]Mod Compatibility[/b][/center][/size][size=3]All optimization mods should be fine, as well as content mods if they don't touch the pre-rendered cinematics.[/size]
[size=6][b][center]Feedback[/center][/b][/size][size=3]If you notice any kind of problem, please report them under this listing.
If the 4k videos aren't showed properly, and you are on the latest update, please make sure you uninstalled the Fix4kCutscenes. The "Thank you" Update of the game fixed the scaling issue.
[/size]

author: Caites
description:
[center][size=5][color=#ffd966][b]Easier or Harder New Game Plus[/b][/color][/size][/center]

[quote][center][size=3][b][color=#b6d7a8]Clair Obscur v1.5[color=#b6d7a8].5[/color] ready[/color][/b][/size][/center]
[/quote][size=3]

Vanilla new game plus adds fixed number of levels to NPCs for every NG finished. Its +60/90/110/130/150 levels in vanilla.
You may found new game plus 1 overly easy with most NPC being oneshoted or overly hard if you haven't explored or grind during first playthrough.

I added a few presets for NG+:
1) +15 levels to vanilla for evern NG+ (harder)
2) +30 level (much harder)
3) -10 levels (easier)

Mind, please, you might want additional HP boost for harder versions. There is [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/mods/77]mod[/url] from other author for that. Try x1.5 or x2 version.[/size]

[color=#ffd966][size=4][b]Installation[/b][/size][/color]
[size=3]Unzip three [i]000_NewGamePlusBaguette_P[/i][/size] [size=3]files from archive to this game folder: \Steam\steamapps\common\Expedition 33\Sandfall\Content\Paks\~mods. Create ~mods folder if you don't have it already.[/size]

[size=3]Path for Gamepass users: C:\XboxGames\Clair Obscur- Expedition 33\Content\Sandfall\Content\Paks\~mods.[/size]

[color=#ffd966][size=4][b]Uninstallation[/b][/size][/color]
[size=3]Just remove three [/size][size=3][i]000_NewGamePlusBaguette_P [/i][/size][size=3]files.[/size]

This mod removes Volumetric Fog, Bloom, Chromatic Aberration, Depth Of Field or Complet Fog.

[center][color=#ffff00][size=5][b]NEW MOD 9/5/25[/b][/size][/color][/center]I have had time to play this game and I have observed that although disabling the Depth Of Field from the options disables other effects and disabling the Chromatic Aberration is not completely disabled throughout the game and remains in many screens as the end of battle, I have created a new mod [b][color=#00ffff]Remove Chromatic Aberration - Depth Of Field[/color] [/b]for me and now I share it with everyone for anyone who wants it.[b]

You don't need to look for folders inside hidden Windows system files like Engine.ini, you can use Vortex for installation and you don't need expert NASA settings.
[/b]
[b][color=#00ff00][size=6][u]IMPORTANT NOTES
[/u][/size][/color][size=4]Only use one file at a time.
[/size][/b]Any modifications to [b]Engine.ini[/b] that modify the three changes made by this mod will overwrite those changes.
This mod does not modify any major aspect of the graphics settings, so all other settings will be changed normally in the game's graphics options.

[b][center][size=6][b]TO REDUCE OF STUTTERING IN MOST OPERATING SYSTEMS
WE RECOMMEND THE WINDOWS REGISTRY EDITOR
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/site/mods/1088]High Priority in CPU - DISK - RAM Processes (Reduces Stuttering in GAMES)[/url][/b][/size][/center][/b]

[b][color=#ffff00][u]INSTRUCTIONS[/u][/color][/b]
Unzip and put the [b].PAK[/b] into the [b][color=#00ffff]~mods[/color][/b] folder.
If the [b][color=#00ffff]~mods[/color][/b] folder does not exist, create it and it will work.
Sandfall\Content\Paks\[b][color=#00ffff]~mods[/color][/b]

author: Bighead
description:
[size=4][b]Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 - Unreal Config[/b][/size]

Creates and configures "Engine.ini" for Clair Obscur: Expedition 33.

[img]https://i.ibb.co/WvJ66nfC/image.png[/img]

[size=4][b]Installation[/b][/size]

The executable can be ran from anywhere, but it is suggested to copy it alongside [b]Expedition33_Steam.exe[/b] for the Steam version, [b]Sandfall.exe[/b] for the GamePass version, or [b]SandFall-Win64-Shipping.exe[/b] for either version. This allows launching the game through this configurator.

[size=4][b]Usage[/b][/size]

- Copy configurator to game directory alongside launch executable.
- Load up the configurator. If no INI exists, it will alert you how to create one.
- If you do not have an INI, create it with [b]File >> Create Engine.ini[/b].
- This will unlock the various options that will affect the graphics of Clair Obscur.
- Configure the game how you like, or use the built in presets from the menu bar.
- Suggested to load a [b]Low-Ultra[/b] preset before using [b]Sharp & Clear[/b] or [b]Soft & Ambient[/b].
- A backup can be created and loaded from the menu bar, allowing test configurations.
- Doubles as a launcher using the [b]Launch Game[/b] button or the menu bar option.
- Can quickly task kill the game from the menu bar option for quick testing.
- Compatible with most premade [b]Engine.ini[/b] files that use [b][font=Courier New][SystemSettings][/font][/b] section.

[size=4][b]Advanced Usage[/b][/size]

- Click the [b]Show Options[/b] radio button under [b]Advanced Options[/b].
- From here a new world opens up in terms of configuration.
- [b]Collections[/b] contains a bunch of predefined settings from other mod authors.
- Copy and paste your own options into the textbox and click [b]Parse[/b] to add them.
- Add, delete, or configure options directly from the grid.
- Parsing values that falls under the "standard" config imports their values.

[size=4][b]Camera FOV[/b][/size]

[img]https://i.ibb.co/qM16G5VZ/image.png[/img]

- This tool can also configure MrHunts [b][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/mods/172][b]Camera FOV[/b][/url][/b] mod with a GUI.
- From the toolbar at the top, select the "[b]Camera FOV[/b]" tab and select "[b]Configure...[/b]". 

[size=4][b]Other Info[/b][/size]

Some options will have a profound effect on graphics, but it's also possible that some options don't have any apparent effect. To make things simple, there are some built-in [b]Presets[/b] that can be selected for a base configuration. If wanting to use one of the bottom two presets (Sharp & Clear/Soft & Ambient), it is suggested to first choose one that reflects the power of your PC (Low - Ultra). From there the settings can be fine tuned. The menu bar Game option can launch the game if it's in the game folder alongside the game's executable, and it can also task kill the game process (this may require administrator privileges). It's also possible that Windows will see this application as some kind of virus as it doesn't like apps that mess with running apps. I assure you that it is not, all the code is visible.

If you wish to use this as a base to create a configurator for another game, feel free to fork the code on Github and do what you like with it. I tried to make it easy to edit, configure, and adapt and heavily commented the code. If you do use it, all I ask for is credit for the base project.

[b][u]GitHub Repo[/u][/b]: [url=https://github.com/BigheadSMZ/ClairObscur-UConfig]https://github.com/BigheadSMZ/ClairObscur-UConfig[/url]

[center][size=5][color=#ffd966][b]Slightly Better Map Menu[/b][/color][/size][/center]

[quote][center][size=3][b][color=#b6d7a8]Clair Obscur v1.5[color=#b6d7a8].5 [/color]ready[/color][/b][/size][/center]
[/quote][size=3]

Mod slightly improves map experience.

Main feature - [color=#ffd966]visibility of level labels on any zoom level[/color]. That will lead to slight overlapping, but its better than vanilla visibility on max zoom only.

Labels [color=#ffd966]text got outline[/color] to make it more distinguishable on white background.

Also [color=#ffd966]boosted pan and zoom speed[/color] a bit, they are too slow for KB/M.

Version for KB/M has faster zoom and panning speed, version for controllers has vanilla map camera controls.[/size]

[color=#ffd966][size=4][b]Installation[/b][/size][/color]
[size=3]Unzip three [i]0000_BetterMapMenu_P[/i][/size] [size=3]files from archive to this game folder: \Steam\steamapps\common\Expedition 33\Sandfall\Content\Paks\~mods. Create ~mods folder if you don't have it already.[/size]

author: Caites
description:
[center][size=5][color=#ffd966][b]Easier Gradient Counter and Jump[/b][/color][/size][/center]

[quote][center][size=3][b][color=#b6d7a8]Clair Obscur v1.5[color=#b6d7a8].5[/color] ready[/color][/b][/size][/center]
[/quote][size=3]

Mod increases immunity durations and decreases lock windows (pressing W or J too early will lock you from next successful parry/jump even when in time) for gradient counter and jump.[/size]

[quote][size=3]Gradient Counter : 1s (lock window aka [b]bad[/b])/0.4s (immunity window aka [b]good[/b]) -> 0.5s/0.9s (half-second longer)
Jump: 0.9s/0.65s -> 0.4s/1.15s[/size][/quote]
[color=#ffd966][size=4][b]Installation[/b][/size][/color]
[size=3]Unzip three [i]0000_GradientAndJumpEasier_P[/i][/size] [size=3]files from archive to this game folder: \Steam\steamapps\common\Expedition 33\Sandfall\Content\Paks\~mods. Create ~mods folder if you don't have it already.[/size]

[color=#ffd966][size=4][b]Uninstallation[/b][/size][/color]
[size=3]Just remove three [/size][size=3][i]0000_GradientAndJumpEasier_P [/i][/size][size=3]files.[/size]

[color=#ffd966][size=4][b]Compatibility[/b][/size][/color]
[size=3]Isn't compatible with any battle base blueprint mods.[/size]
[size=3]Compatible with [/size][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/mods/28]Easier or Harder Dodge and Parry[/url][size=3].[/size]

[color=#ffd966][size=4][b]Known Issues[/b][/size][/color]
[size=3]Some people experience locked free aim and crashes on item use in combat, please, bear that in mind.[/size]

[color=#ffd966][size=4][b]My Mods[/b][/size][/color][size=3]
[size=3][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/mods/20]No Intro[/url]

author: Luzid1ty
description:
[center][size=6][b][color=#00ffff]Lune Misc Outfits – Danseuse & Sakapatate[/color][/b][/size][/center]
[center]
[size=4]These mods replace [b]Lune's and[/b] [i]unintentionally[/i][b] Sciel's outfits[/b] with two new themed outfit: [b]Danseuse[/b] and [b]Sakapatate (Yoruichi-inspired)[/b].
[color=#ff0000][b]Note:[/b][/color] Sciel’s outfits are replaced due to shared body references.[/size][/center]

[center][size=6][b] [color=#f4cccc]Danseuse Outfit (Lingerie)[/color][/b][/size][/center][center][size=4]
Meant as lingerie. Some technical issues delayed the full version, so I'm releasing this version as-is before it gets scrapped. (Wish i could have made it better)
The translucent veil includes basic physics – a bit clunky but still playable.[/size][/center]
[center][color=#ffff00][size=6][b] Sakapatate Outfit (Yoruichi from Jump Force)[/b][/size][/color][/center]
[center][size=4]Inspired by Lune’s [color=#ffff00]Lightning Dance Skill[/color][/size],[size=4] this includes Yoruichi's recognizable outfit from Jump Force and an [i]optional hair piece[/i].
The hair is included as an extra—it’s a little silly but I included it anyways. I considered adding Yoruichi's transformed state(Lightning Bikini) as an alt look, but for now it’s just this version.[/size][/center]

[left][size=5][b]Installation[/b][/size][/left]

1. Then install your desired outfit files:
• [b]Lune[/b] – Danseuse and/or Sakapatate

[b][color=#ff0000][size=5]Installation Path:[/size][/color][/b]
[color=#f4cccc][size=4]SteamLibrary\steamapps\common\Expedition 33\Sandfall\Content\Paks\~mods[/size][/color]
[i]If the ~mods folder doesn’t exist, just create it manually.[/i]

[left][size=5][b][color=#ffff00]Known Issues[/color][/b][/size][/left]
• Sciel’s outfits will be replaced unintentionally due to shared body references
• Minor veil physics bugs (Danseuse set)
• Optional Yoruichi hair may act oddly in motion
• Let me know if anything breaks or needs fixing!
• Clipping

[center][size=4][b]💖 Like the Mod? Support Future Work!
[/b][/size][size=3]If you enjoy the mods and want to keep them coming, consider dropping a tip on Ko-fi! Your support keeps me going.[/size][size=3][/size]

[b][url=https://ko-fi.com/luzid1ty][size=4]👉 ko-fi.com/luzid1ty[/size][/url][/b]
[size=3]Thanks again for all the encouragement and feedback.[/size][/center]

author: RubemJR
description:
[center]﻿COExp33
[size=6][b][color=#f1c232]The Definitive Performance Mod[/color][/b][/size]
Up to 127% Boost on FPS! (Using the Performance ini + optimized settings)
[size=1](Tested on [b][color=#ffd966]2026-04[/color][/b]) - [/size][b][size=1]Compatible with Patch v1.5.5[/size][/b][/center]
[center][b][size=1]ALL OLDER VERSIONS OF THE MOD CAN BE FOUND [url=https://github.com/ru-bem/COExp33-The-Definitive-Performance-Mod/tree/main/Archive]HERE[/url][/size][/b][/center]
[center]
🏆 [b][u][color=#f1c232]1k+ Endorsements![/color][/u][/b] 🏆
[size=1]Thank you![/size]
[s][size=2]----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------[/size][/s]

[color=#f1c232] - This mod is and will be [b]free forever[/b]. -
If this made your game run better, please consider a donation. It helps a lot more than you can imagine!
[b]There's a donation tab above (paypal) and there're these other options below:[/b][/color]

[url=https://ko-fi.com/rubem][img]https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ru-bem/COExp33-The-Definitive-Performance-Mod/refs/heads/main/assets/kofi.png[/img][/url] [url=https://livepix.gg/rubem][img]https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ru-bem/COExp33-The-Definitive-Performance-Mod/refs/heads/main/assets/livepix.png[/img][/url]
[color=#f1c232]
[s][size=2]--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------[/size][/s]
🟡 Thank you for being here since the beginning of this mod! I'll see you again on another update, if needed. [b]🟡[/b]
[/color] [color=#f1c232][size=2][s]--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------[/s][/size][/color]

[b][u][color=#ea9999]AMD[/color] GPU owners:
[/u][/b]This mod will fix most of the visual issues but may not provide a [b]huge[/b] performance boost for you. It's not my fault.
UE5.4 variables don't seem to scale well on some of your GPUs. [b][u]But try it out![/u]

[/b][u][b][color=#b6d7a8]NVIDIA[/color] and [color=#a4c2f4]INTEL[/color] GPU owners:
[/b][/u]Have fun!

[s][size=2]----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------[/size][/s]
[color=#f1c232][size=6][b]Presets[/b][/size][/color]
[size=5]📈[/size]


🟢 Original: [size=4][color=#f1c232][b]+0%[/b][/color][/size]
The game as intended by the developers. Play this way if you have enough performance!

[b]🟰 Lossless: [/b][size=4][color=#f1c232][b]+70%[/b][/color][/size]
The closest possible to the original. This is [i][b]almost[/b][/i] free performance.

[b]⚪ Quality: [size=4][color=#f1c232]+80%[/color][/size]
[/b]Very high quality. Only small visual changes were made for more performance.

[b]⚖️ Balanced:[/b] [size=4][color=#f1c232][b]+105%[/b][/color][/size]
Same as quality but with little less quality lumen and shadows (basically).

[b]⏩ Performance: [size=4][color=#f1c232]+127%[/color][/size]
A miracle[/b]. Expedition 33 fully playable without lumen. Huge performance increase.
[size=1]The Manor lighting becomes weird from here and below. If you don't want that, use Balanced of higher.[/size]

[b]🥔 Potato: [size=4][color=#f1c232]+201%[/color][/size]
[/b]Meant for potato PCs. Focused only on performance.
[size=1]Remember, The Manor lighting is very weird here. If you don't want that, use Balanced of higher.[/size]

[b]💀 Monstrosity: [size=4][color=#f1c232]+261%[/color][/size]
[/b]Hell. Broken visuals and unplayable devilishness. This costs your soul and shouldn't be used at all.

[b]🎮 Steam Deck[/b] [color=#f1c232][size=4][b]Quality[/b][/size][/color]
Optimized for Steam Deck (30fps)
Use with XESS + Low Preset

[b]🎮 Steam Deck [color=#f1c232][size=4]Performance[/size][/color][/b]
Optimized for Steam Deck (45fps)
Use with XESS + Low Preset
⚠️ Expect some visual issues on specific areas of the game using this preset.

📷 Check mod images for comparison

Performance gains may vary depending on your hardware.
This was tested on: [b]R5 3600 + RTX 2060 + 16gb RAM + DLSS 4

[/b]
[quote][b]- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/mods/398][u]Use this mod to fix the FPS drop in combat[/u][/url] -[/b][/quote]
📷 If you want to use the photo mode delete the lines inside the [b]; PProcess[/b] section,
from r.DepthOfFieldQuality=0 to r.Tonemapper.Quality=0. Also delete the line r.SubsurfaceScattering=0

[s][size=2]----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------[/size][/s]


[b][color=#f1c232][size=6]Installation [/size][/color][/b][size=5]
⚙️[/size]

There are [b]two methods[/b] to install the mod. I recommend you to use the [b]automatic[/b] method, which is by far the easiest.
For both methods [u]start by [b]extracting the contents of the zip file[/b][/u].

[size=4][b]Automatic mode[/b][/size]:
[b][u]1[/u][/b] - After extracting the folder, run the "Auto Install" file.
[b][u]2[/u][/b] - Type the number correponding to the preset you want to install, press [Enter] and have fun!

It's a fully commented batch script, so you can open it in a text editor and see exactly what each line of code does.
More details [url=https://github.com/ru-bem/COExp33-The-Definitive-Performance-Mod/blob/main/Automatic%20Install%20-%20The%20Definitive%20Performance%20Mod.bat]here on github[/url]﻿.

[size=4][b]Manual mode:
[/b][/size][b][u]1[/u][/b] - Choose any preset and open it's folder. "2_Quality" for example.
[b][u]2[/u][/b] - Copy all the files inside this preset folder.
[b][u]3[/u][/b] - Press WIN+R, type [b]%LocalAppData%\Sandfall\Saved\Config[/b] and press enter.
[b][u]4[/u][/b] - Look for [u]one of these[/u] two folders: "Windows" or "WinGDK" and open it.
[b][u]5[/u][/b] - Now paste the files you copied here.
[b][u]6[/u][/b] - You now should have 3 files inside this folder: Engine.ini, Scalability.ini and GameUserSettings.ini
[b][u]7[/u][/b] - The files MUST be set as read-only, but they already are. To make sure, right-click the file, choose 'Properties', and check whether ‘Read-only’ is selected.
[b][u]8[/u][/b] - Run the game, go to Graphics options, use the optimized settings (more details below) and have fun!

For Steam deck the folder is: [i][b]/home/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/1903340/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/AppData/Local/Sandfall/Saved/Config/Windows/[/b][/i]

[size=2][s]----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------[/s][/size]


[b][color=#f1c232][size=6]Simplified Optimized Settings[/size][/color][/b]
[size=5]🖥️[/size]

[color=#f1c232]🟡 This mod scales well with in-game presets (Low, Medium, High and Epic).
[b][u]- Feel free to try other combinations if you have a faster or slower setup -[/u][/b][/color]
[color=#f1c232]You can lower Shadows, Gi and Folliage for even better performance.[/color]

🤩 High end GPUs [i](RTX 4070+)[/i]:[b] High preset + Lossless ini
[/b]😎 Mid end GPUs [i](RTX 3060~)[/i]: [b]High preset + Quality ini[/b]
😄 Mid-low end GPUs [i](RTX 2060~[/i]): [b]High preset + Quality ini[/b]
😊 Low end GPUs [i](RTX 3050~)[/i]: [b]Medium preset + Balanced ini
[/b]😐 Very old GPUs [i](GTX 1050~)[/i]: [b]Medium preset + Performance ini
[/b]💀 (GT 1030~): [b]Low preset + Potato ini[/b]

[b]Scaling Type priority order:[/b] DLSS > FSR > TSR > XESS
Keep in mind that there are some tradeoffs between TSR and XESS. Use what looks best to you visually.

[s][size=2]----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------[/size][/s]


[size=6][color=#f1c232][b]Update DLSS, FSR and XESS[/b][/color][/size]
[size=5]⬆️[/size]

[size=4][b]Benefits:[/b][/size]
[size=2]• Much better looking upscaling.[/size]
[size=2]• Less vram usage.[/size]
[size=2]• Less ghosting.[/size]


[size=4][b]How to update:[/b][/size]

[color=#f1c232][b]• Easiest method[/b][/color]

[b]Auto Install[/b][size=2] comes with an disabled option to automatically update DLSS, FSR and XESS, which is [b][color=#e06666]not compatible[/color][/b] with [b][u]v1.5.5[/u][/b] of the game.[/size][/center]
[center][size=2]You can enable it anyways if you want by changing the [b]line 32[/b] of the Auto Install script from [b]uupdater=0[/b] to [b]uupdater=1[/b][/size][/center]
[center][/center]
[center][b]1 - [/b][size=2]Then, open the script, Type [b](A)[/b][/size][b] [/b]and press enter.
[b]2 - [/b]Copy and Paste your game's folder location here then press enter.
[b]3 - [/b]If the folder is valid you'll be prompted to confirm that you want to update. Type [b][1][/b], press enter and wait until the end of the process.

[b]Now the script will do all this for you:[/b]
- Create a folder called DLSS_XESS_FSR on user's temp folder.
- Download the latest DLSS DLL directly from NVIDIA's github page.
- Download the latest XESS DLL directly from INTEL's github page.
- Download the latest FSR DLL directly from AMD's github page.
- Create a backup of the old version.
- Copy each file to the right place.

[i][u]Tested on Windows 11 24H2 and 25H2[/u][/i]


[b][color=#f1c232]• Manual Method[/color]
[/b]
[b]DLSS 4 (Latest):[/b]
[b][u]1 -[/u] [/b][size=2]Download these two DLLs:[/size]
[url=https://github.com/NVIDIA/DLSS/raw/refs/heads/main/lib/Windows_x86_64/rel/nvngx_dlss.dll]https://github.com/NVIDIA/DLSS/raw/refs/heads/main/lib/Windows_x86_64/rel/nvngx_dlss.dll[/url]﻿﻿
[url=https://github.com/NVIDIA/DLSS/raw/refs/heads/main/lib/Windows_x86_64/rel/nvngx_dlssd.dll]https://github.com/NVIDIA/DLSS/raw/refs/heads/main/lib/Windows_x86_64/rel/nvngx_dlssd.dll[/url]﻿﻿﻿
[b][u]2 -[/u] [/b][size=2]Copy them into: [/size][i][gamefolder][/i][i]\Sandfall\Plugins\NVIDIA\DLSS\Binaries\ThirdParty\Win64/[/i]

[b]XESS (Latest):[/b]
[b][u]1 -[/u] [/b][size=2]Download these four DLLs:
[url=https://github.com/intel/xess/raw/refs/heads/main/bin/libxell.dll]https://github.com/intel/xess/raw/refs/heads/main/bin/libxell.dll[/url]﻿﻿
[url=https://github.com/intel/xess/raw/refs/heads/main/bin/libxess.dll]https://github.com/intel/xess/raw/refs/heads/main/bin/libxess.dll[/url]﻿
[url=https://github.com/intel/xess/raw/refs/heads/main/bin/libxess_dx11.dll]https://github.com/intel/xess/raw/refs/heads/main/bin/libxess_dx11.dll[/url]﻿
[url=https://github.com/intel/xess/raw/refs/heads/main/bin/libxess_fg.dll]https://github.com/intel/xess/raw/refs/heads/main/bin/libxess_fg.dll[/url]﻿[/size]
[b][u]2 -[/u][/b] Copy them[size=2] into: [/size][i][gamefolder]/Engine/Plugins/Marketplace/XeSS/Binaries/ThirdParty/Win64/[/i]

[b]FSR 4 (Latest):
[/b][b][u]1 -[/u][/b] Download these two DLLs:
[url=https://github.com/GPUOpen-LibrariesAndSDKs/FidelityFX-SDK/raw/refs/heads/main/Kits/FidelityFX/signedbin/amd_fidelityfx_upscaler_dx12.dll]https://github.com/GPUOpen-LibrariesAndSDKs/FidelityFX-SDK/raw/refs/heads/main/Kits/FidelityFX/signedbin/amd_fidelityfx_upscaler_dx12.dll[/url]﻿
[url=https://github.com/GPUOpen-LibrariesAndSDKs/FidelityFX-SDK/raw/refs/heads/main/Kits/FidelityFX/signedbin/amd_fidelityfx_framegeneration_dx12.dll]https://github.com/GPUOpen-LibrariesAndSDKs/FidelityFX-SDK/raw/refs/heads/main/Kits/FidelityFX/signedbin/amd_fidelityfx_framegeneration_dx12.dll[/url]﻿
[u]2 -[/u] Copy them[size=2] into: [/size][i][gamefolder]/Sandfall/Binaries/Win64/[/i]
[s][size=2]----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------[/size][/s]


[color=#f1c232][size=6][b]Customizations
[/b][/size][/color]Here are some lines that you can add or change to your liking. This will be updated over the time.
[b][u]You can now add them automatically by using the auto-install script![/u][/b]
Or, if you want to do it manually, copy and paste the variables at the very bottom inside any Engine.ini provided by this mod. Inside the "Custom Variables" section.

To be able to edit the file right click on it, choose 'properties', uncheck "read-only" and apply.
Remember to set the file back to read-only after editing.


[i][color=#f1c232][size=4][b][Useful][/b][/size][/color][/i]

[b]Increase Saturation:[/b]
[quote]r.Color.Grading=0[/quote]Default is =1


[b]Reduce fog:[/b]
[quote]r.VolumetricFog.VoxelizationShowOnlyPassIndex=1[/quote]Default is =-1  |  Higher = Less fog


[b]Increase View Distance:[/b]
[quote]r.ViewDistanceScale=2[/quote]Impacts performance  |  Requires a game or save reload  |  Lower = more performance + more pop-ins

[i]
[color=#f1c232][size=4][b][Fun][/b][/size][/color][/i]

[b]⚠️ Epilepsy Warning:[/b]
[quote]r.AntiAliasingMethod=0
r.Color.Mid=0.2
r.Color.Grading=0
r.EyeAdaptation.LensAttenuation=0
r.Lumen.Reflections.SpecularScale=150
r.Lumen.ScreenProbeGather.Temporal=0
r.SkylightIntensityMultiplier=0[/quote]
[b]Inverted World (Pretty cool):[/b]
[quote]r.Lumen.Reflections.SpecularScale=50
r.SkylightIntensityMultiplier=1000
r.Color.Mid=0.3[/quote]
[b]Low Poly World:[/b]
[quote]r.Nanite.MaxPixelsPerEdge=1e8
r.SkeletalMeshLODBias=10
r.MipMapLodBias=10[/quote]
[b]Can it run EXP33? [/b]- Huge FPS drop
[quote]r.Nanite.MaxPixelsPerEdge=0[/quote][s][size=2]----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------[/size][/s]

[b]Your feedback is appreciated![/b]

What changed in the Auto-Install script:

• Various small visual fixes for the script.

Thank you so much for using this mod!

files.changelog_html:
• All INIs: Changed the value of r.Shadow.RadiusThreshold to 0.04 (game's default on high preset). This should fix the white hair/beard for the remaning people.
• Performance and Potato INI: Now the overall lighting is much better on all regions except for The Manor. It's not my fault.
• Performance and Potato INI: Removed some redundant variables.
• Potato INI: Way less pop-ins now.
• Auto-install script: Now you can also update the FSR DLLs through the script.
• Auto-install script: Added a "Reduce fog" option inside the Custom Vars menu because "Remove fog" causes issues for some people.
• Updated the description and mod files to mention FSR when needed.

Technical stuff:
• Auto-install script:
- on TDPM v1.6.0+ you could, in theory, revert an INI back to it's defaults after applying any custom variable through the Custom Vars section by pressing [R]. This is actually working now.
- Improved some parts of the code, more specifically inside the ini_custom, install_custom_r and installini labels.
- TDPM v1.6.0+ if you choose an custom var and go back to the menu, the selected INI will not be highlighted. This is fixed now.
- Minor UI changes.

files.changelog_html:
• All INIs (Except Steam Deck): Updated the sharpening and denoiser variables to better work with the new DLSS 4.5 (310.5.0)

• Remember:
- You can update to DLSS 4.5 using the Upscaler Updater inside the script, or via Nvidia App.
- You can adjust the sharpening by changing the value of the line r.Tonemapper.Sharpen=0.3 (Default is =1, but it's too much)

• Auto-install: Fixed some typos and changed the name of the [A] option to 'Upscaler Updater'.
• Monstrosity INI: Very minor update.

files.changelog_html:
- On the new game's update (1.5.5) newer upscalers DLLs (DLSS, XESS, FSR) seem to be incompatible. Because of this, the "Update Upscaler" is now disabled in the script by default. You can enable it again if you want by changing line 32 of the code. 1 = Enabled, 0 = Disabled.
- Made some very minor adjustments to almost all "Scalability.ini" files to counter the performance loss that came with this new update by giving you around 3~4 extra FPS.
- This new update also made the game much sharper, so I've updated all "Engine.ini" files to bring this effect back to 0.

author: Matyas Mustoha
description:
Magyar fordítás: Mustoha Mátyás


[b]Tudnivalók[/b]

A fordítás a játék jelenleg legfrissebb változatához készült (1.3.1, 2025-06-11), ettől eltérő verziókkal nem garantált a működés.

A fordítás az angol feliratot cseréli le. Ha eddig más nyelven játszottál, telepítés után ne felejtsd angolra állítani a játékon belül a nyelvet.


[b]Telepítés[/b]

1. Keresd meg a játék mappáját
2. Ezen belül lépj a `Sandfall/Content/Paks/` mappába
3. Másold ebbe a mappába az összes mellékelt fájlt

[i]Megjegyzés[/i]: Ha nem tudod, hogy hol a játék mappája, gyorsan odaugorhatsz, ha megnyitod Steamen belül a játék adatlapját, majd jobb oldalt, a fogaskerék gomb menüjében a *Kezelés → Helyi fájlok böngészése* opciót választod. Angol nyelvű felület esetén ez a *Manage → Browse local files* névre hallgat.


[b]Eltávolítás[/b]

Egyszerűen töröld ki az összes mellékelt fájlt.


[b]Jogi információk[/b]

A fordítás a szerző szellemi tulajdonát képezi. A fordítást módosítatlan formában (ezzel a dokumentummal együtt) bárkinek ingyenesen továbbadhatod. Mindig fel kell tüntetned a fordítót, mint szerzőt, és az eredeti forrást, ahonnan beszerezted a fordítást. Nem használhatod üzleti célokra a fordítást a szerző előzetes írásbeli engedélye nélkül. A szerző nem tehető felelőssé a fordítás használatából adódó esetleges károkért.

Other launchers might have different structure.

description:
This mod adds transparent glasses to (nearly) every Lune hairstyle. It also adds her "Wavy" hairstyle back into the game.
I also have mods that add glasses to [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/mods/618]Verso[/url], [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/mods/539]Gustave[/url] and [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/mods/730]Sciel[/url].


[list]
[*][size=4][b]"Gestral"[/b] hairstyle is replaced with [b]"Rebellious White"[/b] with glasses.[/size]
[*][size=4][b]"Baguette"[/b] hairstyle is replaced with default [b]"Lune"[/b] with glasses.[/size]
[*][size=4][b]"Simon"[/b] hairstyle is replaced with [b]"Wavy"[/b] with glasses.[/size]
[*][size=4][b]"Sirene"[/b] hairstyle is replaced with [b]"Ponytail"[/b] with glasses.[/size]
[*][size=4][b]"Osquio"[/b] hairstyle is replaced with [b]"Esquie"[/b] with glasses.[/size]
[*][size=4][b]"Bald"[/b] hairstyle is replaced by the [b]"Wavy" [/b]hairstyle.[/size]
[/list]
[center][size=3][b]
"Lune"[/b], [b]"Rebellious White"[/b], [b]"Esquie"[/b], [b]"Ponytail"[/b] and [b]"Clea"[/b] hairstyles remain unchanged.[/size][/center]

An optional file is included to replace the "Bald" hairstyle with "Wavy" without altering any other hairstyle, which can be installed separately
from the main file.


To install, place the files in [u]\Expedition 33\Sandfall\Content\Paks\~Mods[/u]. if the ~Mods folder does not exist, create one.

Join the [url=https://discord.gg/63KsUxBVvw]Expedition 33 Modding Server[/url] if you are so inclined!


[b][size=4]If you want to support me, tips are appreciated <3[/size][/b]

[url=https://ko-fi.com/lungblossom][img]https://i.ibb.co/CKDZ60jy/kofi1.png[/img]

[/url]

author: Chic enthusiasts
description:
[b]General Mod Information[/b]

- Sciel and Lune no longer share the same mesh using the Chic outfit - the meshes are differentiated so that they more accurately resemble the original Lune and Sciel skins in terms of proportions.
- More color variants for the Chic outfit
- Sciel and Lune can use different color of the Chic outfit

[b]Installation
[/b]
- For each Lune and Sciel you can choose between using a Semi-transparent "Lace dress" and a non-transparent "Non-lace dress". Then you can choose between the dress having long or short sleeves and boots being or not being a part of the outfit. Finally you can choose from several color options.

- For more information about combining various files from this mod check the Readme file inside the mod ZIP file.

- Choose maximum one mod (=subfolder) from each of the folders named Lune color/Lune outfit/Sciel color/Sciel outfit

- Copy either the whole subfolder or the files within (ending in "_P") into the "...Expedition 33\Sandfall\Content\Paks\~mods" folder.

- If you want to only change colors for Sciel and Lune using the vanilla outfit use only the "_only Separate colors_P" mod (in the "Non-lace dress" folder) and choose colors for Lune/Sciel from the Lune color/Sciel color folders. Vanilla outfit will not work with the colors from the Lace dress version of the outfit.

- As of version 3.3.1 I consider this mod finished. I have no further plans to add or change anything. Even as a request.


[b]_________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________[/b]

[b]Only Separate colors mod[/b]

- Added a mod (on mod page in "Optional files" category) that allows assigning different color to Sciel and Lune even without changing the outfit. Use this mod only when changing nothing but the colors for default outfits. If you want to change color only on Lune, you will use a color for her from the "Lune color" subfolder, but also have to give Sciel her default color by using "Sciel_chic_default_P" from the subfolder "Sciel color". The color mods are in the main mod file.


[b]_________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________

v3.3.1[/b]

- Final tweaks regarding clipping issues. Mostly for the Lace version of the dress.

[b]_________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________[/b]

[b]v3.3
[/b]
- Added two more color variants in form of a Lace version of the dress with some transparency.
- Older outfit files were updated to work alongside the Lace versions (old outfits on Sciel, if only Lune was to use the new Lace outfit, would have broken textures without updating the files)
- Sciel's body is now oiled up a bit to accentuate existing details.

[b]_________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________[/b]

[b]v3.2[/b]

- Added option to choose different color variant for Lune and Sciel (if Sciel's modded outfit is used, a color has to be used aswell for proper function)
- Added 3 color variants - Fire, Galaxy, 33 Décembre

[b]_________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________

v3.1

[/b]- Sciel's mod now doesn't replace her Bikini outfit and is equipped via ...Chic outfit. Thus non-Chic outfits are no longer affected.
- Some minor tweaks
- Some clipping issues are still present in infrequent animations, but removing those without making mess elsewhere is now almost impossible

[b]_________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________[/b]

[b]v2.2

[/b]- Mod no longer affects other swimsuit skins which now work properly. But modded Sciel's Chic skin still replaces her Bikini outfit.
- Main mod archive now includes all the Color variants.
[b]_________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________[/b]

[b]v2.1[/b]

- Minor tweaks to the original modded outfits with sleeves. 
- Added version of the outfit without sleeves and with some extra tweaks - can be applied independently for Lune/Sciel.
- Added versions of the outfits with/without shoes - can be chosen independently for Lune/Sciel.
- Added 3 color variants - doesn't have to be applied, but the color will apply both for Lune and Sciel (ie. no blue Sciel and white Lune).

[b]_________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________[/b]

[b]Other use case scenarios[/b]

- If only Sciel mod is used, Lune's Chic skin will remain the vanilla one
- If only Lune mod is used, Sciel's Chic skin will be broken (textures), unless the appropriate (as in for the version of the modded outfit Lune is using) "_only Lune mod_fix_P" mod is also used. Then Sciel will share Lune's mod with correct textures.
- You can use just the color mod to change the color of vanilla Chic outfit.


As such, the way the mod works/can work is that Sciel either has to have her own modded outfit, or she will share the same outfit as Lune. It doesn't work the opposite way - Lune can't have Sciel's modded outfit, but can have vanilla Chic while Sciel is using a modded one.

author: kxperience
description:
[center][img]https://kxperience.de/media/Banner1.png[/img]

[font=Arial][color=#bf9000][size=6]IN PROGRESSION [/size][size=6]![/size][/color][/font]




[youtube]vacGTvgqGVA[/youtube]




[youtube]JPWPuVduqkk[/youtube]




[youtube]ZD8LJ86SmWo[/youtube][/center]




[center][img]https://kxperience.de/media/Pic1.png[/img][/center]


[center][font=Times New Roman][color=#ff7700][size=5][u][b]Worum geht's?[/b][/u][/size][/color][/font]

[font=Arial][color=#999999][size=2][b]Vollständige deutsche Synchronisation aller Charaktere, inGame-Dialoge und Zwischensequenzen.[/b][/size][/color][/font]
﻿﻿
[color=#ff7700][font=Times New Roman][size=5][u][b]Beschreibung[/b][/u][/size][/font][/color]

[font=Arial][color=#999999][size=2][b]Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 bietet leider keine offizielle deutsche Audio-Lokalisation. Deutschsprachige Spieler mussten sich daher auf Untertitel beschränken und sich zwischen der hervorragenden französischen oder englischen Originalvertonung entscheiden. Mit diesem ambitionierten Projekt, wird dieses grandiose Spiel um eine vollständige deutsche Sprachausgabe erweitert. Jeder Dialog und jede Zwischensequenz
wurde Satz für Satz, 'per Hand' herausgearbeitet -[/b][/size][/color]

[size=3][b]Keine automatisierte Stapelverarbeitung![/b][/size]
[b][color=#999999]
Ursprünglich als privates Projekt gedacht, wurde schnell klar, dass es mit eigens dafür entwickelter Software, etwas größer werden würde. Heraus kam eine akribisch gestaltete Sprachumsetzung auf Deutsch. [/color][/b][color=#999999][b][size=2]Ziel war und ist es, die Persönlichkeiten aller Charaktere korrekt einzufangen, jeden Dialog und jede Cutscene, [/size][size=2]nah am Original und dennoch völlig für sich selbst-stehend, natürlich und mit sinngemäßer Betonung ins Deutsche zu übertragen. [/size][size=2]Ich hoffe mir ist das annährend gut gelungen und bin sehr neugierig auf euer Feedback und konstruktive Kritik.[/size][/b][/color]
﻿[/font]





[font=Times New Roman][b][color=#ff7700][size=5][u]Status und Roadmap[/u][/size][/color]

[font=Arial][color=#efefef][size=4]Version 0.9.1[/size][/color][/font][/b][/font]

[font=Arial][color=#999999][size=2][b]Die Umsetzung dieses Langzeit-Projekts ist ungefähr zu 90% fertiggestellt und wird schrittweise veröffentlicht, [/b][/size][/color][color=#999999][b][size=2]regelmäßig aktualisiert und beständig weiterentwickelt. [/size][/b][/color]

[/font]

[b][size=4]UPDATE 0.9.1  [Komplettierung Akt 2][/size][/b]


[b][color=#999999][size=2]- Alle noch fehlenden 'Cinematics' -> Aktfinale (Endkampf Renoir / Malerin etc.), wurden dem Spiel hinzugefügt
- Alle noch fehlenden 'Dialog_Sequences' -> Dialoge im Lager, GestralVillage (Golgra), wurden dem Spiel hinzugefügt
- Alle noch fehlenden 'InWorld_Dialogue' -> Sprachausgaben in den Gebieten und der Oberwelt, wurden dem Spiel hinzugefügt
- Alle noch fehlenden 'BattleDialogue' -> Storysätze (Renoir, Malerin etc. während des Kampfes), wurden dem Spiel hinzufgefügt
- Sämtliche 'BattleLines' von Gustave -> Kampf- und Loot-Kommentare, wurden überarbeitet und ersetzt
- Sämtliche Freundschafts / Beziehungs / Side-Quest - Dialoge (*außer Esquie & Verso) wurden dem Spiel hinzugefügt[/size]
[/color]



[size=4]Noch zu erledigen:[/size]

[color=#999999][size=2]- Alle Cinematics von Akt 3
- Alle noch fehlenden Side-Quests und Secrets
- Gesamte Freundschafts / Beziehungs - Dialogreihe von Esquie & Verso
(beide Stimmen bereiten mir aktuell noch große Kopfschmerzen)
- Integration aller noch fehlenden Tagebücher sowie der Austausch aller bestehenden durch neu-überarbeitete Versionen
- Sämtliche überarbeiteten 'BattleLines' (Kampf- und Loot-Kommentare) von Lune, Maelle, Monoco, Sciel und Verso[/size][/color]


[color=#ff7700][size=2][Hinweis: Weiterhin gilt alles als "NICHT FINAL". Es gibt besonders bei den Freundschafts / Beziehungs - Dialogen noch jede Menge 
Korrekturbedarf. Diese Korrekturen werden direkt im nächsten Update, on the fly, miteinfließen.][/size][/color][/b]




[font=Arial][size=4][b]Übersetzte Audio-Dateien:[/b][/size][/font]

[b][size=3][font=Arial][color=#00ff00]Prolog: 100% vollständig[/color] [color=#00ff00]✓[/color]
[color=#00ff00]Akt 1: 100% [/color][font=Arial][color=#00ff00]vollständig [/color][/font][color=#00ff00]✓ [/color]
[color=#00ff00]Akt 2: 100% vollständig [/color][/font][font=Arial][color=#00ff00]✓[/color][/font][/size][font=Arial]

[color=#bf9000][size=3]Akt 3: 85% [/size][/color][/font]

[/b]



[font=Times New Roman][color=#ff7700][size=5][u][b]Installation[/b][/u][/size][/color][/font]
[b][font=Arial]


[size=4]Option 1 - VORTEX - Mod-Manager[/size]

[font=Arial][color=#999999]Diese Mod ist mit Nexusmods Hauseigenem Mod-Manager '[u]VORTEX[/u]'[size=2] sehr einfach und unkompliziert zu installieren. [/size][/color]
[size=2][color=#999999]Einfach Das Zip-Archiv via Drag & Drop nach Vortex ziehen und auf[/color] [/size][size=3]Install All[/size][size=2] [color=#999999]klicken. Fertig![/color][/size][/font][/font][/b][/center]
[b][font=Arial]
[color=#999999][img]https://kxperience.de/media/tut1.png[/img]﻿[/color][/font]
[/b]
[center][b][font=Arial][size=4]Option 2 - Benutze den Installer[/size]

[color=#999999][size=2]Entpacke das Zip-Archiv. Darin befindet sich folgende Datei-Struktur mit diesen Dateien:[/size][/color]
[img]https://kxperience.de/media/Dateistruktur.png[/img]﻿
[size=2][color=#999999]Doppelklicke die Datei "[/color][color=#00ff00]COE33-DE-SynchroMod-Installer.exe[/color][color=#999999]", wähle das Spielverzeichnis,[/color]
[color=#999999] dass automatisch gefunden und angezeigt wird wenn Du [/color][/size][color=#d8d8d8][size=2]Clair Obscur: Expedition 33[/size][/color][color=#999999][size=2] installiert hast. Fertig![/size][/color]



[size=4]Option 3 - Manuelle Installation[/size]

[color=#999999]Für die manuelle Installation einfach den Ordner "[/color][color=#ff7700]Content[/color][color=#999999]" aus dem entpackten Zip-Archiv [/color][/font]
[font=Arial][color=#999999]in den Ordner '[/color][color=#b6b6b6][size=2]Sandfall'[/size][/color]
[/font]
[size=2]\Steam\steamapps\common\Expedition 33\Sandfall\[/size]

[color=#999999][font=Arial][size=2]des Spielverzeichnisses kopieren. Fertig![/size][/font][/color]





﻿
[/b][color=#ff7700][font=Times New Roman][size=5][u][b]Support[/b][/u][/size][/font][/color]

[font=Arial][color=#999999][b]Wenn Dir dieses Projekt gefällt dann unterstütze mich gerne und empfehle es auch Anderen Freunden
aus der Clair Obscur Community weiter und teile es natürlich auch mit...[/b][/color]

[i][color=#999999][size=2].[/size]..all [/color][color=#b6b6b6][size=3]Jenen[/size][/color] [color=#d8d8d8][size=3]d[/size][/color][color=#d8d8d8][size=3]ie[/size][size=4] [/size][size=5]nach[/size][/color][size=5] [/size][color=#efefef][size=6]uns[/size][/color][size=5] [/size][size=6]kommen[/size][/i][/font]

description:
[quote][b][size=5][b][color=#ffff00]If a fight feels tedious or impossibly tanky, you’re probably facing it too early or without the right tools for the job.[/color][/b][/size][/b][/quote][b][size=5][b][color=#ffff00]
LATEST NEWS (2026-04-10): version 1.13 released!!![/color]
[/b][/size][color=#00ff00][size=5][b][u]The mod now covers a complete story play through!!![/u][/b][/size][size=3][b]
Many thanks to: [url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/heavylog81]heavylog81[/url], [url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/ResNikTion]ResNikTion[/url], [url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/Rickdaily12]Rickdaily12[/url] for beta testing and everyone in the comments section for the feedback. [/b][/size][/color]
[size=4][b][color=#ffff00]
Channels currently streaming the mod: [url=http://twitch.tv/nafeasonto]Twitch(6:00PM EST daily)[/url]﻿, [url=https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLPbhYfeRvnhm2js6e10ixIxRdlYOHI9CD&si=GXHwqOJB7sOfUe_-]Youtube (every Friday)[/url]﻿, [url=https://www.twitch.tv/kamiineyko]Twitch[/url][/color][/b][/size][/b]
[b][size=4][color=#ffff00]Veteran playthrough guide:  [url=https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLyu7gAHftAvEV50tW6RRv3a2ehFbvThGx]RickDaily12[/url][/color][/size][/b]
[b][size=4][color=#ffff00]
CHANGELOG:[/color][/size]

[size=4][color=#00ff00]Act 3:[/color]
Act 3: [color=#ffff00]Citizens in Lumiere are Strong archetype now (they were Regular). [/color]Mod covers a complete walk-through (see the intended order in the  [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/articles/26]article[/url]). You can also play half of Endless Tower 1-1 to 5-3 (lvl 61 to lvl 75) before going to Lumiere. [/size]

[color=#00ff00][size=4]Note about enemies' HP: Due to the wide variety of builds players can use in Act 3, enemy HP (even when higher than in the base game) may still not be sufficient to challenge the overpowered builds you’re accustomed to. Use the challenge menu to adjust enemy health to your preference.[/size][/color]
[size=4]
[color=#00ff00]Act 1 and 2:[/color]

- Fixed Maelle's Tutorial bug
- Fixed Clair bug in the Monolith[/size][/b]
[b][size=4]- Fixed Benisseur still re-spawning[/size][/b]
[b][size=4]- Reduced parry windows for jumping and gradient attacks:[/size][/b]
[quote][b][size=4]Jump 0.65s -> 0.4s (same as vanilla gradient)
Gradient 0.4s -> 0.22s (same as vanilla dodge)[/size][/b][/quote][b][size=4]Note: If you want more extreme values check the mod [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/mods/737]Extremist Difficulty[/url][/size][/b]

[b][color=#00ff00][size=4]LevelScalingPatch:[/size][/color][/b]
[color=#00ff00][size=4][b] [/b][/size][/color]
[color=#ffffff][size=4][b]This patch increases enemy speed scaling at higher levels (60, 70, 80, and 90), at values midway between the current version (1.13) and the infamous 1.10 version. It must be placed next to the the main mod.[/b][/size][/color]
[b]
[color=#ffff00]NOTE: Act 3 has been particularly challenging to balance due to the wide variety of viable party builds. The mod is designed around high damage-output strategies (marking, defenceless, powerful, pictos, etc.), but it is not balanced for one-shot builds that stack all buffs into a single opening attack. If enemies feel too weak for your preferred playstyle, I recommend using the challenge multipliers to compensate.

There’s also a technical limitation: some encounters—especially in places like Frozen Hearts—are limited to just three enemies, and Chromatics can only bring along small or thin companions. So yeah, you might not see the same overwhelming packs from Acts 1 and 2… but don’t get comfortable. I’ve given Chromatics new ways to fight back—and they’re not playing fair this time 😏 You’ll see.[/color]
[size=4]
In Act 1 and Act 2, all encounters were tested with a reasonably strong, mid-tier team (not min-maxed) to keep fights challenging without turning them into a slog. If an encounter feels tedious or overly tanky, it likely means you’re tackling it earlier than intended or without using the right weaknesses/strategy.[/size]
[/b]

Each update includes adjustments to Acts 1 and 2. You can safely install new versions during an ongoing playthrough, as they typically contain minor fixes for Act 1. It is recommended to stay up to date with the latest version of the mod.

Featured feedback of the week:
[/b][/size][/b][/size]
[quote][size=4][b][size=3][b]﻿rip this is now harder than silksong, defo lives up to its name, great for learning every enemies parry windows, I didn't even know half these enemies did half the things they do; as normally u just blow them all up in 1 turn XD[/b][/size][/b][/size][/quote][size=4][b][size=3][b]

[color=#00ff00]IMPORTANT: [/color]There are no longer "trash mobs." Every encounter is meant to matter. [b][size=3][b]You may find that your initial encounters with certain enemy types and bosses may take multiple attempts or several more minutes than what you are accustomed to. However, as you learn the patterns and develop a good strategy, that time will naturally go down and you should find the "sweet spot" that makes each encounter satisfying without feeling too grindy.[/b][/size][/b] Check some of the gameplay [url=https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLMd3wH9_L-H-0YPIhOVvkUsPeE_fTMkM0]videos[/url] for reference ([color=#ff0000]spoilers alert[/color]), and read the comments section as there are very useful tips there.

To keep fights efficient, remember to prioritize the following:
[/b][/size][/b][/size]
[list]
[*][size=4][b][size=3][b]Elemental & Weapon Weaknesses: [/b][/size][/b][size=3]Knowledge from your first playthrough is essential, so [b][color=#ffff00]don’t hesitate to swap party members when they’re not suited for the area[/color].[/b] I ditched Gustave for Sciel in Stone Wave Cliff XD — and Sciel never saw the light of day in the Forgotten Battlefields.[/size][/size][/*]
[*][size=4][b][size=3][b]Multiplier Stacking: [/b][/size][/b][size=3]Keep maintaining Buffs (Powerful/Rush) on your party and applying Debuffs (Defenseless/Slow) to enemies most of the time.[/size][/size][/*]
[*][size=4][b][size=3][b]AP Economy & Parrying:[/b][/size][/b][size=3] Perfect Parries are your primary source of Action Points and counterattacks do a lot of damage. If you are not parrying consistently, you will fall behind the intended damage curve.[/size][/size][/*]
[*][size=4][b][size=3][b]Status Synergies:[/b][/size][/b][size=3] Do not ignore **Burn**. This status effect has been scaled to remain highly effective against the increased HP pools.[/size][/size][/*]
[/list][size=4][b][size=3][b]
Your feedback is greatly appreciated, especially to help tweak or address any encounters that feel unfair or impossible. [color=#ffff00]Please note that some encounters are intentionally designed to be skipped and revisited later.[/color] We can discuss the mod in the Discord [url=https://discord.gg/tPmr2nAdMN]channel[/url]. If you start from the Prologue, disable tutorials during the Maelle fight. You can re-enable them after that encounter.
[/b][/size][size=3]
[size=3]Please take a look to the [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/articles/22]Design Philosophy[/url] behind the mod, but here is a glance:[/size][/size]
[/b][/size]
[quote][size=4][b][size=3][size=3][size=3]To maintain consistency with the original story and game progression, I followed these rules when designing encounters:[/size][/size][/size]

[/b][size=3][size=3][size=3]- Add as many enemies as possible while prioritizing variety and avoiding repetition.
- Enemies should come from the pool associated with that area, unless the base game already includes enemies from other areas.
- Chromatics should not fight alone; they should preferably be accompanied by another Chromatic or regular enemies (depending on the arena layout).
- Chromatics from the same Act can be mixed and relocated, as the base game already does this.
- For World Map encounters, I respect the enemies visible roaming in the field, ensuring they appear in the encounter; however, additional enemies not shown may also be present.
- Prioritize enemies that buff or synergize with each other through complementary abilities.[/size][/size][/size][/size][/quote][size=4][b]
[size=5]------------------------------------------

No Pushover Enemies: Combat Overhaul[/size]
[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][size=4][size=3]Are you tired of enemies melting in one turn?[/size][/size][/*]
[*][size=4][size=3]Do you miss the intensity and adrenaline of the [b]EndlessTower Challenges[/b]?[/size][/size][/*]
[/list][size=4][size=3]
This mod transforms Act 1 and 2 in Expert difficulty into a real battlefield; where every encounter demands attention, strategy, and skill. Watch some [url=https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLMd3wH9_L-H-0YPIhOVvkUsPeE_fTMkM0]gameplay[/url] (spoilers)

After trying multiple playthroughs, early game combat started to feel predictable. Enemy patterns were easy to read, encounters became routine, and the thrill of battle disappeared. I wanted the main game to feel like the Endless Tower from the very beginning:[/size][/size]

[list]
[*][size=4][size=3]Dangerous enemy combinations[/size][/size][/*]
[*][size=4][size=3]Tactical fights that keep you thinking[/size][/size][/*]
[*][size=4][size=3]Battles that reward mastery instead of autopilot[/size][/size][/*]
[/list][size=4][size=3]
[size=3]In the base game you'll soon find out that you actually don't need any strategy. I wanted to have time to in one turn to buff, in other to setup the conditions, and in a third to execute my OP attack. But in the base game everything die too fast (except bosses).[/size]

So I rebuilt Act 1 and 2 encounters from the ground up using [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/mods/443]The Expedition 33 Randomizer[/url] by Hastur1325.[/size]

[b][size=3]IMPORTANT: This mod is designed for a second playthrough, starting from scratch and with the knowledge of your first playthrough. [color=#ffff00]It won't work for a current save file pass the first flag in Meadows. [/color]You can use any save file from the prologue though. [b][size=3][b][color=#ffff00][b][size=3][b]Also, you can use any of [b][b]the optional save files[/b][/b] to start directly from the Festival or Spring Meadows.[/b][/size][/b][/color][/b][/size][/b] [/size][/b]

[b][size=3][b][color=#ffff00]Regarding farming difficulty: there is actually no need to farm at all. If you start from the Meadows and defeat all regular enemies, you should reach the end of Act 1 at around level 22 and arrive at Monoco’s Station at about level 25. [/color][color=#00ff00]Eventually, your entire party should reach around level 48 before the Paintress (even while skipping most Chromatic encounters) and start Act 3 at level 50-52.[/color]

[/b][/size][b]You can install the [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/mods/200]Place Name Level Indicator[/url] to give you an idea of the expected level in each zone.
[/b][/b][/size]

[b][size=5]What ﻿to Expect[/size][/b]

[size=3]This is NOT a simple stat increase.

Every change was designed to create fair but intense combat:[/size]

[list]
[*][size=3]Rebalanced enemy health, attack power, and speed.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Adjusted some enemy's skills to create more dynamic encounters.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Tweaked encounter compositions for strategic depth.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Enemies actually fight back.[/size][/*]
[/list][size=3]
The difficulty of the mod comes mainly from the enemy composition in each encounter. Enemy stats have been slightly adjusted (roughly 10%–33%), mostly to better align with buff/debuff values. Health received the largest increase, sometimes ranging from 1.5x to 3x. In the base game, unless you play very poorly, enemies tend to become trivial and fights feel like a breeze — often ending before you even see most of their attacks.

Enemy damage has also been modestly increased (around 20%), but enemies should never one-shot you. Remember that there are debuffs, such as [b]Powerless[/b], that help counter incoming damage. You can check the [size=3][size=3][url=https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLMd3wH9_L-H-0YPIhOVvkUsPeE_fTMkM0]gameplay[/url][/size][/size]  videos to get a sense of the intended pacing: normal encounters should last around 3–5 minutes, while bosses/chromatics typically take about 10 minutes — or up to 15 if you make a lot of mistakes XD.

Because XP sources are now more limited, it’s encouraged to rotate your party depending on the zone you’re in. This helps prevent characters from falling behind in levels, but more importantly ensures you’re using the right strengths against specific enemy types.[/size]
[size=3]
No more ONE-TEAM-TO-RULE-THEM-ALL[/size]
[b]
[size=5]Hidden Surprises[/size]
[/b]
[size=3]I also added several gameplay tweaks to spice things up .No spoilers here. Discover them yourself during your playthrough.[/size]

[b][size=5]Feedback Welcome[/size][/b]

[size=3]This is an evolving overhaul. Your feedback helps shape future updates. Tell me:[/size]

[list]
[*][size=3]Which encounters pushed you the hardest[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]What felt fair or unfair[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]What you want to see next[/size][/*]
[/list]
[b][size=5]Known Issues:[/size]

[/b][size=3][size=3]In some early fights, because the positioning of some enemies, the camera will move to a place where an object can obstruct partially your vision, like a rock or some leaves. This also happens in the base game with some encounters where the camera pull out and goes too down, that the grass block your vision. This is very rare, and not game breaking in my opinion. Consider it as part of the "added difficulty".

[b][size=5]Install Instructions:[/size][/b]

Just extract the content of the zip file in: `H:\Gaming\GOG Games\Expedition 33\Sandfall\Content\Paks\~mods`[/size][/size]


[b][size=5]FAQ:[/size]

[size=3]Please take a look to the [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/articles/22]Design Philosophy[/url] behind the mod for further information.[/size][/b][size=3]

[b]1. Why doesn’t it work with existing save files?

[/b]You need to start a new game because enemies behave differently in this overhaul (no spoilers 😉). It’s required that you haven’t killed any enemies in Acts 1 or 2.
I’ve uploaded a save file in Slot 6 at the Festival so you can jump in right before reaching the Continent.

[b]2. Are there any conflicts with other mods?
[/b][/size]
This mod is incompatible with:

* Mods that change zone levels (e.g., High Lvl Lumiere — Lumiere is set to level 69 in this mod).
* Mods that alter leveling pace (XP required per level).
* Mods that modify enemy stats or encounter compositions.
* Mods that modify merchants inside zones.
[size=3]
Aside from those, everything else should be compatible (skill descriptions, zone level titles, .ini edits, etc.).

[b]3. Why can't I farm anymore? [/b]

The game is fully playable without farming. In my own playthrough, I reached Forgotten Battlefields at level 22, Monoco's station at level 25 and Sirene at level 37, I even had to leave some Chromatic enemies alive to return later 😅.

[b]4. Good for you, but I still want farm XP to get OP early!

[/b]Your game, your rules. You can still farm XP by repeating vendor fights, using the Hidden Arena, or revisiting Chromatic enemies.

[b]5. Why are enemies having so many turns?[/b]

Use Slow and Rush strategically, and consider equipping Pictos with high Speed bonuses.

[b]6. I found an encounter that I think is too difficult for the level/skills I have?

[/b]Don’t feel bad about skipping encounters — that’s intentional. In my own playthrough, I had to skip some fights and return later once Maelle joined the party to defeat the Chromatic Lancelier in Spring Meadows.[/size]
[b]
[size=3][b]7. I don't believe you! Chromatics are impossible to beat at any level![/b][/size][/b][size=3]

Chromatics are designed to be defeated when you return to earlier areas with the Obstacle Breaker ability (rewarded after finding 4 lost Gestrals). To demonstrate this, I’ve uploaded [size=3][url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MKkuKRBdA8A]videos[/url] [/size]on my channel showing that, even with mistakes, they can be defeated with the right strategy.

[b]8. Was the new name change inspired by RE9?

[/b]Partially; because I haven’t had the chance to play RE9 yet because I’ve been focused on this mod 😅The name was mainly inspired by [b]Skyrim’s Requiem[/b], one of my all-time favorite mods and the one that originally got me into [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrim/mods/87289]modding[/url].[/size]

Perfect for players who felt NG+ or a second playthrough lacked challenge.

author: arancetta
description:
This mod gives Maelle the Civilian outfit from her ending, as well as the paint flowing down her face.

The mod has two parts that can be installed separately, [b]CivilianPatate_P[/b] will add the outfit and [b]MaellePainter_P[/b] will add the hairstyles.

[size=3][b]"Sakapatate"[/b] outfit has been replaced with [b]"Civilian"[/b]. It will make Maelle appear taller than usual.[/size]

[list]
[*][size=4][b]"Gestral"[/b] hairstyle has been replaced with [b]"Ponytail White"[/b] with the face paint.[/size][/*]
[*][size=4][b]"Gestral White"[/b] has been replaced with [b]"Ponytail White"[/b] with the face paint and a painted right eye.[/size][/*]
[*][size=4][b]"Ponytail White"[/b] remains unpainted.[/size][/*]
[/list]
When using this mod, [b]"Osquio"[/b] hairstyle will also include the paint on her face. The icons of the outfits and hairstyles in the wardrobe menus are unchanged.

The mod should work in all cutscenes that allow customization, but let me know if you run into any issues! Do not start fighting about the ending in these comments.

To install, place the files in[u] \Expedition 33\Sandfall\Content\Paks\~Mods[/u]. if the ~Mods folder does not exist, create one.


Join the [url=https://discord.gg/63KsUxBVvw]Expedition 33 modding server[/url]﻿ if you are so inclined!


[b][size=4]If you want to support me, tips are appreciated <3[/size][/b]

[url=https://ko-fi.com/lungblossom][img]https://i.ibb.co/CKDZ60jy/kofi1.png[/img][/url]

author: arancetta
description:
This mod adds transparent glasses to (nearly) every Sciel hairstyle. It also adds Lune's "Wavy" hairstyle for her, with and without glasses.
I also have mods that add glasses to [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/mods/618]Verso[/url], [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/mods/539]Gustave[/url] and [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/mods/654]Lune[/url].

The glasses may slightly clip into her temples in certain angles. hopefully it's not too noticeable, i just thought the slightly smaller frames looked the best on her.


[list]
[*][size=4][b]"Gestral"[/b] hairstyle is replaced with [b]"Messy Bun White"[/b] with glasses.[/size]
[*][size=4][b]"Baguette"[/b] hairstyle is replaced with default [b]"Sciel"[/b] with glasses.[/size]
[*][size=4][b]"Osquio"[/b] hairstyle is replaced with [b]"Esquie"[/b] with glasses.[/size]
[*][size=4][b]"Simon"[/b] hairstyle is replaced with [b]"Wavy"[/b] with glasses.[/size]
[*][size=4][b]"Bald"[/b] hairstyle is replaced by the [b]"Wavy"[/b] hairstyle.[/size]
[/list]
[b]
[/b][center][size=3][b]"Sciel"[/b], [b]"Messy Bun White"[/b], [b]"Esquie"[/b] and [b]"Clea"[/b] hairstyles remain unchanged.[/size][/center]

An optional file is included to replace the "Bald" hairstyle with "Wavy" without altering any other hairstyle, which can be installed separately from the main file.


To install, place the files in [u]\Expedition 33\Sandfall\Content\Paks\~Mods[/u]. if the ~Mods folder does not exist, create one.

Join the [url=https://discord.gg/63KsUxBVvw]Expedition 33 Modding Server[/url] if you are so inclined!


[b][size=4]If you want to support me, tips are appreciated <3[/size][/b]

[url=https://ko-fi.com/lungblossom][img]https://i.ibb.co/CKDZ60jy/kofi1.png[/img]

[/url]

author: Frexod
description:
This is my first mod release ever so I’m still new to this, feedback is always welcome


[size=4][b]What this fixes:[/b][/size]

[list]
[*]Removes black spots / blotches on other outfits when using skimpy skins edits made by [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/users/162857458]TiberiusGracco[/url]  or nude body mods made by [url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/Luzid1ty?gameId=7635]Luzid1ty[/url] since they use custom body with custom textures[/*]
[/list][b][size=4]Installation[/size][size=3]:[/size]

[size=3]    - Option 1 ( Standard Install ):[/size]
[/b]
[list]
[*]Download the Base Fix (required)[/*]
[*]Download the outfit fixes you want ( multiple outfit fixes can be used at the same time )[/*]
[*]Place all files into: Sandfall/Content/Paks/~mods[/*]
[/list][size=3][b]    - Option 2 ( AIO Install ):
[/b][/size]
[list]
[*]Download and extract into: Sandfall/Content/Paks/~mods[/*]
[*]Each fix is included in a separate folder[/*]
[*]ONLY keep the fixes for the skins you are using and delete everything else for example: If you are using mods that replace Expedition Outfit and Skirt Outfit, only keep Expedition Fix and Skirt Fix folders, and delete all other fix folders[/*]
[*]There are multiple Expedition Outfit Fix versions included, use ONLY ONE depending on the skin you are using, Do NOT install more than one at the same time[/*]
[*]If for some reason outfit fixes are not working, try moving the Pak files directly into the ~mods folder (not inside any subfolders)[/*]
[/list]
[b][size=4]Compatibility:[/size]
[/b]
[list]
[*]Works with skimpy skin edits made by [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/users/162857458]TiberiusGracco[/url] [/*]
[*]Works with nude body mods made by [url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/Luzid1ty?gameId=7635]Luzid1ty[/url][/*]
[*]May not be compatible with mods that replace or override the same materials or textures[/*]
[*]Mods that use different material order may require separate fixes[/*]

author: RedFoxtail26
description:
[b]Update 4/9/26[/b]: Today's hotfix provides an official patch for the bug, making this mod obsolete! Please go update the game instead.
Thanks to everyone who used this in the interim; it really means a lot :)

The current vanilla patch has a bug causing only the newest burn instance to deal damage. This returns to the old stacking behavior, while keeping the new damage calculation.

To install, unzip the mod file and place the .pak, .ucas, and .utoc files in your game folder under Sandfall/Content/Paks. To uninstall (e.g. when the official fix arrives), delete/move those three files.

author: TeXas and Marwan Ayman
description:
[b]Description
[/b]
Yes, it’s real. Yes, it’s fully edited and integrated. And yes, you [i]will[/i] hear it every time your team dies.

This initially started as an inside joke between me and a music producer friend of mine, originating from mishearing the game over sound [b]"Au Clair Obscur"[/b] as [b]"Bomboooclat Obscur"[/b] (don’t ask us how that happened).
Then it escalated into: [i]"What if we actually mod this into the game?"[/i]
He produced and edited the audio… and I held up my end of the deal.
And here we are.
[line]
[b]Features[/b]
[list]
[*]Replaces the death sound with a fully custom voice line [/*]
[*]Cleanly edited and mixed for in-game use [/*]
[*]Keeps the timing and feel of the original audio… with a hint of bomboclat [/*]
[/list][line]
[b]Installation Instructions[/b]
[list]
[*]Simply head to the game's main directory [/*]
[*]Extract the files into:

[code]Sandfall \ Content \ Paks \ ~mods[/code]
[i](If you don’t have a ~mods folder, manually create one.)[/i] [/*]
[/list][line]
[b]Credits[/b]
🔊 Sound made by [b]Marwan Ayman[/b]
YouTube: [url=https://www.youtube.com/@MarwanAyman3]https://www.youtube.com/@MarwanAyman3[/url]
Instagram: [url=https://www.instagram.com/marwanayman3/]https://www.instagram.com/marwanayman3/[/url]

Special thanks to [b][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/profile/Elemor99/mods]Elemor99[/url][/b] for guiding me through the final packaging steps of this mod! without them, this mod wouldn’t have made it to this page!
name: Bomboclat Obscur - Game Over Sound
summary: This mod replaces the original battle death voice line “Au Clair Obscur” with a custom-made version that announces: “Bomboclat Obscur.”
