author: Taiiden
description:
[size=3]Les valeurs de ce mod proviennent soit directement du code du jeu, soit de tests approfondis réalisés par la communauté. Elles sont représentatives des dégâts qui sont réellement infligés dans le jeu. Le mod ne change aucune valeur numérique dans le jeu et ne fait que les afficher.

[/size]
[size=6]Installation:[/size]

[size=3]Choisissez [u]UNE[/u] version du mod, téléchargez le .zip
Ouvrez les fichiers du jeu sous [size=3][i]Expedition 33\Sandfall\Content\Paks\ [/i](Sur [i]Steam[/i], clique droit, Gérer, parcourir les fichiers locaux)
[/size]Créez un dossier ~mods si ce n'est pas déja fait et placez les 3 fichiers contenu dans le .zip à l'intérieur du dossier ~mods

[/size][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/7635/images/750/750-1776280884-1764100250.png[/img]

[size=6]Informations:
[/size]
[size=3]Le pourcentage de dégâts indiqué en premier sur chaque sort correspond au pourcentage de la puissance du personnage, ce sont les dégats de base.
Les pourcentages de dégâts supplémentaires suivants sont tous calculés en fonction des dégâts de base du sort.
Le nombre entre crochets indique le multiplicateur de dégâts totaux infligés à la barre de Fracture.

Exemple 1 :[/size] [size=3]200% de dégâts Néant en 1 coup. 50% de dégâts supplémentaires si la cible est affectée par brulûre. [0,2x][/size]

[size=2]Les dégâts infligés seront de types "Néant" à hauteur de 200 + ( 0,5 x 200 ) donc 300% de la puissance. Et les dégâts de fracture seront de 0,2 x 300 donc 60% de la puissance.
[/size]
[size=3]Exemple 2 : 125% de dégâts Feu en 5 coups. 200% de dégâts supplémentaires en consommant un pigment de glace. [2x]

[/size][size=2]Les dégâts infligés seront de type "Feu" à hauteur de 125 + ( 2 x 125 ) donc 375% de la puissance. Et les dégâts de fracture seront de 2 x 375 donc 750% de la puissance.

[/size]Pour plus d'informations sur le fonctionnement des formules mathématiques utilisées par le jeu, référez vous au mod original : [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/mods/134?tab=posts]Enhanced Descriptions[/url]

[size=5]Crédits:

[/size]This mod contains assets from an original mod made by [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/profile/Kimjsd]Kimjds[/url]
Un grand merci à [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/profile/WushuLate]WushuLate[/url] pour son aide dans la conception de ce mod. Sa version -> [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/mods/282]FR - Enhanced Descriptions - Skills Pictos and Weapons[/url]

author: Lysril
description:
[size=3]To install, unpack the mod and copy "[b]engine.ini[/b]" to  C:\Users\[b]YourUsername[/b]\AppData\Local\[b]Sandfall[/b]\Saved\Config\Windows.[/size]

[size=3]About two patches ago, Sandfall updated the game's executable to no longer create engine.ini. So no need to worry about duplicate .ini overwriting one another. The .ini is set to read only, because the game deletes the .ini after launch for some reason. Just turn off read only in windows properties to edit the .ini and turn it back on when your done. [/size]

[size=3]Older versions of the game have their own engine.ini, in those cases. Just copy the lines from my mod and paste them in the existing .ini with notepad and save it.[/size]

[size=3]I recommend using XESS set to "anti-aliasing", don't bother with any other options that lowers game resolution. FSR looks "sharper", but has a ton of visual artifacts. TSR is also sharp, but has a ton of ghosting on moving objects. (Every TSR game is like this, maybe that's why the force motion blur in unreal engine games?) DLAA or whatever Nishita has in this game is probably better than XESS, which is the games kind of default AA. Honestly, XESS sucks too and I cannot believe I rather have standard TAA, but we don't in this game. So XESS or DLAA. Hair is still pixelated atrocities and there's no solution, because "modern optimisation" uses shitty dithered hair instead of purehair or TressFX, I rather pay the gpu tax for good hair than this abomination. [/size]

[size=3]If want bloom or depth of field, just edit the .ini and set the 0 to 1. Delete the "ScreenColorFringe" lines if you want to restore the games original colour grading. [/size]

[size=3]Also, I am not stating FSR is bad or a FSR hater, it's just bad in this game. I am not arc or intel user either. I don't care which corporation does what, only what hardware works and settings work "best" for me.[/size]

[size=3][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/images/7635/149016043-1776361383.png[/img][/size]

author: Sonweb4
description:
This is a simple re-adding of Maelle's ACT3 outfit already in the game. (Not good with physics/stiff)

First and probably only mod I will ever do. I just wanted to see if I could reimplement the outfit already in the game, akin to what [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/clairobscurexpedition33/users/80314278]Demojay[/url] does. But he uses the BP I think, and I'm adding the same skin on top of the outfit already there. I was able to get it to look the same, after having some texture issues. But I couldn't figure out how to get the outfit to actually use clothing physics, and after spending a few nights on just getting the outfit to appear properly... I am tired; it was alittle fun, the trials and errors.

Anyway, I wanted to show my work, even if its buns, its still my effort. If you can fix it, or make your own version, go right on ahead.

Have fun countering and dodging.

author: ZEDDTAY
description:
[size=5][b]Description[/b][/size]
Tired of watching the mandatory ~7 second "Expedition Failed" animation every time you lose a battle? This mod uses a custom Lua script to detect the game over screen and dynamically accelerate game time (TimeDilation), bypassing UI delays and showing the Retry/Load menu instantly.

[size=5][b]Main features[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][b]Instant Skip:[/b] The failure logo and animation pass in less than 0.5 seconds.[/*]
[*][b]Engine Safe:[/b] Uses internal UE5 logic, making it compatible with NVIDIA Frame Generation and DLSS.[/*]
[*][b]Stateless Polling:[/b] Designed to work consistently across multiple game sessions without memory leaks or crashes.[/*]
[/list]
[size=5][b]Installation[/b][/size]
[list=1]
[*]Install [b]RE-UE4SS (Experimental/Rolling version)[/b] into your [i]Sandfall/Binaries/Win64[/i] folder.[/*]
[*]Download this mod and extract the [b]ue4ss[/b] folder from the ZIP into your [i]Sandfall/Binaries/Win64[/i] directory.[/*]
[*]If you have other Lua mods, ensure [i]SkipDeathMod : 1[/i] is present in your [b]mods.txt[/b].[/*]
[/list]
[size=5][b]Requirements[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][b]RE-UE4SS[/b] (Mandatory for Lua scripts to work).[/*]
[/list]

files.description: Extract the ZIP into Sandfall/Binaries/Win64 folder. Requires UE4SS.
files.name: Instant Death Skip - V1.0

name: Instant Death Skip and Fast Retry (UE4SS)
summary: Skips the mandatory ~7-second "Expedition Failed" animation and logo. Get back to the battle menu instantly. Powered by UE4SS.

author: dragoncosmico
description:
This preset uses my own shaders and Zenteon free shaders:

[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/site/mods/1280]HDR Simulation[/url]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/site/mods/804]ColorExposure Shader[/url]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/site/mods/1410]TonePack[/url]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/site/mods/1334]Sharpening Shaders Pack[/url]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/site/mods/1376]Night Saturation Threshold[/url]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/site/mods/1823]Bloom Based Shader Pack[/url]

//
//
//

[b]Installation:[/b]

Install last Reshade with no shaders.
Extract preset into game folder. Overwrite.

//
//
//

[b]Configuration:[/b]

Start the game (YOU MUST BE IN-GAME AND NOT IN THE MENU) and hit the HOME key for the Reshade overlay to show up.
Enable the DisplayDepth shader.

Now, if you can see the left half of the screen in weird colors and the right side in greyscale then it already works,
turn off DisplayDepth and enjoy.


If you see a complete black screen, click on Add-ons at the top of the Reshade window and you will see all the depth buffers,
you must click on each one of them until you can see the left half in weird colors and the right half on greyscale or black. If you can see the half-half color-grey then go back to Home and turn off the DisplayDepth shader. Done.

If you see half of the screen white and half cyan, go to the Add-ons tabs and enable "Copy depth buffer before clear operations". Done.

Now, if what you see is a black right side, go to the Home section, disable the Performance Mode, click on "Edit global processors definition", change the value of RESHADE_DEPTH_IS_REVERSED from 0 to 1, and  click on "Edit global processors definition" again.
That should do the trick, enable the Performance Mode again and disable the DisplayDepth shader. Done.

//
//
//
name: Surreal RT Simulation
summary: Surreal colorful filter with Ray Tracing simulation
